channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey unveils intercontinental ballistic missile program with 6,000 km range
item.description: Turkey displayed a model of the Yildirimhan ICBM at SAHA 2026 defense expo, a conventionally-armed missile that could reach most of Europe and Asia, marking a significant shift in NATO ally strategic capabilities.

item.title: 🛸 DARPA seeks containerized drone swarm systems for autonomous operations
item.description: DARPA issued a request for concepts for containerized systems that can launch, recover and support drone swarms of up to 500 UAVs, designed for deployment behind enemy lines with minimal human involvement.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Car Bomb Kills 15 Police Officers
item.description: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a checkpoint in Pakistan late Saturday night, killing at least 15 police officers in a devastating attack.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary's New PM Apologizes to Those Wronged Under Orbán
item.description: Péter Magyar, who swept away 16 years of Viktor Orbán's rule, struck a radically different tone in his first speech as Hungary's new prime minister.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia's One Nation Party Wins Historic Parliamentary Seat
item.description: Pauline Hanson's right-wing One Nation party gained its first seat in Australia's lower house in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist party.

item.title: 🇻🇦 Marco Rubio Meets Pope Leo Amid Tensions With Trump Over Iran War
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio held a meeting with Pope Leo, the first American pontiff, who has been a firm critic of the Iran war and the administration's immigration policies.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Starmer Faces Leadership Challenge as 40 Labour MPs Call for Resignation
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under mounting pressure with about 40 Labour MPs reportedly calling for him to quit as Angela Rayner and Wes Streeting eye leadership bids.

channel.item: 🇵🇱 Poland Finds Military Spy Drone Near Russian Kaliningrad Border https://kyivindependent.com/drone-with-cyrillic-markings-found-near-polish-russian-border/ Polish military police discovered a reconnaissance drone with Cyrillic markings near the border with Russia's Kaliningrad exclave. Officials say the drone had no combat capabilities but was likely used for intelligence gathering. Kyiv Independent Europe 2026-05-10T21:00:00+03:00 https://kyivindependent.com/drone-with-cyrillic-markings-found-near-polish-russian-border/

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Sent Response to US War Proposal via Pakistan, State Media Reports
item.description: Iran conveyed its response to a US proposal to end the conflict through Pakistani channels, according to Iranian state media. Pakistan has served as an intermediary in diplomatic communications between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇨🇳 US Sanctions Nine China and Hong Kong Entities Over Iran Military Links
item.description: The US Treasury and State Department imposed sanctions on mainland China and Hong Kong entities accused of enabling Iran's military procurement efforts. The move comes days before Trump's summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Allegedly Built Secret Military Outpost in Iraq for Iran Operations
item.description: Reports indicate Israel established a clandestine military base in Iraq to support operations against Iran. The US reportedly advised Iraq to avoid the area where the facility was allegedly constructed.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran responds to US ceasefire proposal as Gulf states report drone attacks
item.description: Tehran has sent a response to Washington via mediator Pakistan regarding a 30-day ceasefire extension and Strait of Hormuz reopening, while drone strikes hit cargo ships off Qatar and were repelled by Kuwait and UAE.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin says Ukraine conflict is coming to an end
item.description: The Russian leader expressed optimism about potential Ukraine negotiations but criticized Western backing for President Zelensky, while aides cautioned it remains a long road to peace.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel escalates Lebanon strikes, killing 39 despite ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 39 people, including an infant, as Israel and Hezbollah continue trading fire despite a ceasefire deal announced last month.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan car bomb attack kills 15 police officers
item.description: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a checkpoint in Pakistan late Saturday night, killing 15 police officers in one of the deadliest attacks on security forces this year.

item.title: 🇨🇩 DR Congo militia attack kills at least 69 civilians
item.description: CODECO militia fighters killed at least 69 people in an attack in Ituri province, northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo, in one of the deadliest massacres in the region this year.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin leads scaled-down Victory Day parade, vows victory in Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin declared confidence in victory during Moscow's Victory Day parade, which for the first time in nearly two decades featured no tanks or heavy military equipment. Security was tight amid a US-brokered ceasefire.

item.title: 🚢 WHO chief travels to Tenerife for hantavirus cruise ship evacuation
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in Spain to oversee the evacuation of over 140 passengers and crew from a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship heading to the Canary Islands. Three people have died and five are infected in the outbreak.

channel.item: 🇺🇸 Trump feuds with allies likely to outlast Iran war, analysts warn https://www.reuters.com/world/china/trumps-feuds-tensions-with-allies-likely-outlast-iran-war-2026-05-09/ Trump's words and deeds have revived fears among Washington's long-standing friends that the United States might be unreliable in a future crisis, with diplomatic tensions expected to persist even after the Iran conflict ends. Reuters Diplomacy 2026-05-09T15:05:00+03:00 https://www.reuters.com/world/china/trumps-feuds-tensions-with-allies-likely-outlast-iran-war-2026-05-09/

item.title: 🇨🇳💻 China unveils worlds first dual-core quantum computer
item.description: Chinas Hanyuan-2 quantum computer operates at room temperature, marking a breakthrough in quantum computing accessibility and maintenance.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇳🇿 Japans Mogami-class warship gaining ground in New Zealand defense competition
item.description: Japans Mogami frigate and Britains Type 31 are top contenders for New Zealands naval modernization, with Australia potentially influencing the outcome.

item.title: 🇺🇦🤖 Ukraine deploys AI-powered turrets against fiber-optic drones
item.description: Ukrainian forces successfully deployed autonomous AI-driven turrets capable of intercepting Russian drones immune to electronic warfare.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure amid Hormuz clashes
item.description: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action over diplomacy as US forces disabled two Iranian tankers and fired on more Iranian vessels. Qatari PM urges negotiations as ceasefire holds despite ongoing exchanges.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary prepares for regime change as Magyar set to become PM
item.description: Péter Magyar is being sworn in as Hungary new prime minister after his Tisza party won a landslide victory, ending 16 years of Viktor Orbán rule. The new government inherits a grim economic situation and promises asset recovery investigations.

channel.item: 🇧🇭🇮🇷 Bahrain Arrests 41 People Linked to Iran's IRGC https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/bahrain-says-it-has-arrested-41-people-linked-irans-irgc-2026-05-09/ Bahrain's interior ministry announced the arrest of 41 individuals it says were connected to Iran's Revolutionary Guards, amid heightened regional tensions following the US-Iran conflict. Reuters Middle East 2026-05-09T13:03:00+03:00 https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/bahrain-says-it-has-arrested-41-people-linked-irans-irgc-2026-05-09/
